title: Phase 3: Auto-Drafting, Collaboration & Commercial Logic

requirements.text: Implement a 2D GA Drawing Generator that takes the current 3D building model state (dimensions, bay spacing, roof pitch, purlin/girt positions, bracing layout, anchor bolt locations) and renders four standardized 2D SVG views: (1) Anchor Bolt Plan showing pedestal locations and column reactions, (2) Cross Section showing clear height and width with member labels, (3) Roof Plan showing purlin spacing and bracing locations, (4) Side Elevation showing bay spacing and wall bracing. Package all four views into a downloadable PDF file titled 'General Arrangement (GA) Drawings'. Use a client-side SVG-to-PDF approach (e.g., jsPDF + SVG rendering) since server-side Python/matplotlib is unavailable. Add a 'Download GA Drawings' button in the engineering panel or export section of the ProjectViewer.

quotes: Implement a module that takes the 3D model state and renders standardized 2D Vector views (SVG/PDF).
Views Required: Anchor Bolt Plan, Cross Section, Roof Plan, Side Elevation
generate a downloadable PDF package titled 'General Arrangement (GA) Drawings'

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Download GA Drawings' generates and downloads a PDF file
PDF contains all four views: Anchor Bolt Plan, Cross Section, Roof Plan, Side Elevation
Views are dimensionally accurate relative to the current building parameters
Anchor Bolt Plan shows pedestal/column positions with reaction labels
Cross Section shows clear height and width with member annotations
Roof Plan shows purlin spacing and X-bracing layout
Side Elevation shows bay spacing and wall bracing diagonals

requirements.text: Implement LOD 350 high-fidelity connection detail hotspots on the 3D model. Place clickable sphere 'node' markers at key structural joints: Rafter-to-Column Haunch (both ends of each rafter), Ridge Connection (apex), and Base Plate (bottom of each column). When a node is clicked, open a modal dialog showing a high-resolution Three.js or SVG detail view of that connection type. Base Plate detail must show: anchor bolt layout (4-bolt pattern), stiffener plates, and a grout gap between plate and concrete. Haunch detail must show: flange plates, bolt rows (2×4 grid), and web stiffeners. Also render Concrete Pedestals (grey box geometry) and Foundation Blocks (larger grey box below grade, partially clipped) beneath each column base in the main 3D scene to visualize the steel-to-civil interface.

quotes: Place clickable 'nodes' at key structural joints (e.g., Rafter-to-Column Haunch, Ridge Connection, Base Plate)
Base Plate: Anchor bolt layout, stiffener plates, and grout gap
Haunch: Flange connection bolts and stiffeners
Visualize the Concrete Pedestals and Foundation Blocks below the columns

acceptanceCriteria: Hotspot spheres are visible at Haunch, Ridge, and Base Plate joints in the 3D scene
Clicking a hotspot opens a modal with the corresponding connection detail
Base Plate modal renders anchor bolt layout, stiffener plates, and grout gap
Haunch modal renders flange bolts and web stiffeners
Concrete Pedestal geometry is visible below each column in the main 3D view
Foundation Block geometry is visible below each pedestal, partially below ground level
Hotspot nodes are clearly distinguishable (e.g., glowing amber spheres) from structural members

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model for SharedLinks. Each SharedLink record must store: a unique hashed token (e.g., 8-char alphanumeric), the source projectId, the ownerPrincipal, and a createdAt timestamp. Add backend endpoints: createSharedLink(projectId) → returns the token, getProjectByShareToken(token) → returns project data in read-only form (no mutation access). Store SharedLinks in stable backend state.

quotes: Generate a unique, hashed URL (e.g., pebsim.com/share/x9z2p) that loads the model in 'Read Only' mode
Database schema updates for Comments and SharedLinks

acceptanceCriteria: createSharedLink returns a unique alphanumeric token for a given projectId
getProjectByShareToken returns the project data when given a valid token
getProjectByShareToken returns an error/null for invalid tokens
SharedLink records are persisted across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model for Comments (3D Annotations). Each Comment record must store: a unique ID, the projectId it belongs to, the author principal, the target element identifier (e.g., 'column-2'), the 3D world-space position (x, y, z floats), the comment text, and a createdAt timestamp. Add backend endpoints: addComment(projectId, elementId, position, text) → returns the created Comment, getComments(projectId) → returns all comments for a project, deleteComment(commentId) → removes a comment (owner or project-owner only).

quotes: Implement a 'Sticky Note' system. Users can double-click on any 3D element to leave a comment
Database schema updates for Comments and SharedLinks

acceptanceCriteria: addComment persists a comment with element ID, world position, and text
getComments returns all comments for a given project
deleteComment removes the comment if called by the comment author or project owner
Comment records survive canister upgrades

requirements.text: Implement the frontend Public Share view. Add a React route at /share/:token that loads the project via getProjectByShareToken. This view renders the full BuildingSimulator in read-only mode: all editing controls (side-panel inputs, branding controls, engineering inputs, etc.) are hidden or disabled. Show a 'View Only' badge in the header. Add a 'Share' button in the ProjectViewer (authenticated view) that calls createSharedLink and displays the generated share URL in a copyable dialog.

quotes: Generate a unique, hashed URL that loads the model in 'Read Only' mode (no editing controls)
Allow Sales Engineers to send a live link to the client

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /share/:token renders the 3D building model
No editing controls are visible or accessible in the share view
A 'View Only' badge is shown in the header
The 'Share' button in ProjectViewer generates and displays a copyable share URL
Invalid tokens show an appropriate error message

requirements.text: Implement the 3D Annotation (Sticky Note) UI in ProjectViewer. When the authenticated user double-clicks on a structural element (column, rafter, etc.) in the 3D scene, show a small input popover anchored near the click point to type a comment, then submit it via addComment. Render all existing comments for the project as floating 3D billboard markers (e.g., a yellow sticky-note icon + author initial) positioned at their stored world-space coordinates. Add a collapsible CommentSidebar panel listing all annotations with element reference, author, text, and a delete button (for own comments). The share/read-only view shows markers and sidebar in read-only mode (no add/delete).

quotes: Implement a 'Sticky Note' system. Users can double-click on any 3D element to leave a comment
React components for AnnotationMarkers and CommentSidebar

acceptanceCriteria: Double-clicking a structural element opens a comment input popover
Submitting the popover creates a sticky-note marker at the clicked position in 3D space
All comments for the project are loaded and rendered as 3D billboard markers on mount
CommentSidebar lists all annotations with element name, author, and text
Users can delete their own comments from the sidebar
Share view shows markers and sidebar without add/delete controls

requirements.text: Implement the Commercial Costing Engine. Add a 'Rate Card' settings panel (accessible via a tab or modal in ProjectViewer) with four numeric inputs: Primary Steel ($/MT), Secondary Steel ($/MT), Sheeting ($/sqm), and Erection Labor ($/sqm). Persist these rates in local component state (no backend persistence required). Add a `calculateProjectCost` utility function that multiplies each rate against the corresponding calculated quantities from `calculateBuildingStats` (primary steel weight in MT, secondary steel weight in MT, total sheeting area in sqm, floor area in sqm for labor). Display the computed 'Total Project Cost' as a formatted currency value in the engineering sidebar panel, updating reactively whenever building dimensions or rate card values change.

quotes: Rate Card Input: A settings panel where the user inputs current market rates: Primary Steel ($/MT), Secondary Steel ($/MT), Sheeting ($/sqm), Erection Labor ($/sqm)
As the user changes the building size, the 'Total Project Cost' should update in real-time in the sidebar

acceptanceCriteria: Rate Card panel shows four editable numeric fields with appropriate labels and units
Changing any rate value immediately recalculates and updates the Total Project Cost display
Changing building dimensions (span, length, height) recalculates cost in real-time
Total Project Cost is displayed in the engineering/sidebar panel in formatted currency (e.g., $1,234,500)
Cost calculation uses quantities from calculateBuildingStats (weights and areas)

requirements.text: Implement Advanced Export options for STAAD.Pro (.std) and IFC (BIM) formats. Add an 'Export' section in ProjectViewer (e.g., in the engineering panel or a dedicated Export tab) with two buttons: 'Export STAAD.Pro (.std)' and 'Export IFC (.ifc)'. The STAAD.Pro export generates a plain-text .std file encoding the center-line node coordinates and member connectivity (JOINT COORDINATES and MEMBER INCIDENCES sections) from the current building geometry. The IFC export generates a minimal IFC 2x3 text file encoding IfcColumn and IfcBeam entities with their center-line geometry. Both exports trigger a browser file download.

quotes: Add a feature to export the center-line geometry to .std (STAAD.Pro format) or .ifc (BIM format) so engineers can use it for actual structural analysis

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Export STAAD.Pro (.std)' downloads a valid .std text file
The .std file contains JOINT COORDINATES and MEMBER INCIDENCES sections derived from the building geometry
Clicking 'Export IFC (.ifc)' downloads a valid IFC 2x3 text file
The IFC file contains IfcColumn and IfcBeam entities with center-line coordinates
Both exports reflect the current building's dimensions and bay configuration

constraints: No WebSockets or Supabase Realtime — real-time sync requirement must be omitted or replaced with polling if needed; do not implement WebSocket-based live sync
No server-side Python/matplotlib — all drawing generation must be client-side (SVG + jsPDF or equivalent)
All backend logic must remain in the single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo)
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Real-time WebSocket or Supabase Realtime sync between engineer and client views (platform limitation)
Server-side PDF rendering via Python or external microservices
Rust, JavaScript, or any non-Motoko backend language
Integration with Tekla software directly — only file format export is in scope
